title: KnownIssuesFeed
description: The live extension of the knowledge map (GET /api/v1/feed). Clients pull the WHOLE feed periodically and match locally (ad-blocker-list pattern) — never a per-incident lookup. Two sections in one envelope: `issues` (the legacy KnownIssue list, shape unchanged for back-compat) and `data` (the published process-identity corpus). `data` is SIGNED: `signature` is an Ed25519 detached signature over the canonical JSON of `data`, verified client-side against the pinned public key matching `key_id` before any entry grounds a card. signature/key_id/signed_at are null when no signing key is configured. Clients feature-detect the `data` section rather than gating on schema_version.

issue_url.description: Evidence link — cards link evidence, never assert it.

data.description: The published process-identity corpus (approved DaemonProfiles projected via toCorpusEntry) — one shape shared with a corpus/entries/{platform}/ file and the discovery response. Signed by the signature/key_id/signed_at fields below. Values are nullable because a profile may not have every field filled.

process_name.description: The on-disk executable/bundle identity. Entry identity is (process_name, platform).

safe_action.description: HUMAN prose (the enum is mapped to a sentence for the feed); null means no safe intervention exists (tier-3).

sources.description: Citations grounding the entry. On a diagnosis card these collapse to a single link to the reviewed /corpus page — raw URLs are never shown.

source.description: Trust tier the client labels the entry with: anomalous (Haiku-verified), reviewed (human), community (human-reviewed PR).

signature.description: Base64 Ed25519 detached signature over the canonical JSON of `data`. Null when no signing key is configured (clients that require signatures reject a null-signature feed).

key_id.description: Identifier of the public key that verifies `signature` (first 16 hex of its SHA-256); clients pin it.
